- `backend/` (likely a server/API)

- "Run the backend server"
- "Start the shop app"
- "Launch the admin panel"

Alternatively, I can check for common startup files like `package.json`, `main.py`, or other executable scripts if you'd like me to explore further.

Perfect! I can see this is a React Native/Expo project. Let me check the package.json to see the available scripts and then start the shop app for you.

{
  "name": "fixbhai-shop-app",
  "version": "1.0.0",

  },

I can see this is an Expo React Native project. To run the shop app, I can execute:

[RUN_CMD]
npm start "/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app" && ls -la "/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app"

$ npm start "/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app" && ls -la "/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app"
npm error code ENOENT
npm error syscall open
npm error path /Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/Cycy-Clone/goingupdate/package.json
npm error errno -2
npm error enoent Could not read package.json: Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/Cycy-Clone/goingupdate/package.json'
npm error enoent This is related to npm not being able to find a file.
npm error enoent
npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /Users/ajitprajapati/.npm/_logs/2026-02-21T18_21_20_462Z-debug-0.log
[exit code: 254]

I see the issue! The npm command is looking for package.json in the wrong directory (it's still trying to run from the Cycy-Clone directory). Let me navigate to the shop-app directory first and then run the start command properly:

[RUN_CMD]
cd "/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app" && npm start

$ cd "/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app" && npm start
> fixbhai-shop-app@1.0.0 start
> expo start

Starting project at /Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app
› Port 8081 is running this app in another window
  /Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app (pid 83392)
Input is required, but 'npx expo' is in non-interactive mode.
Required input:
> Use port 8082 instead?
› Skipping dev server
[exit code: 1]

I see that port 8081 is already being used by another instance of your app. The Expo development server is asking if you want to use port 8082 instead, but since we're running in non-interactive mode, it can't get your input.

Let me try starting it with a different port explicitly:

[RUN_CMD]
cd "/Users/ajitprajapati/Projects/FixBhai/shop-app" && npm start -- --port 8082
